_note: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for defect taxonomy. Every user-reported defect becomes an entry: root cause -> immediate fix -> PREVENTION (hard gate in the loop) -> detection check (QA rubric) -> extension tag. The extension's TAGS array and the QA subagent rubric are REGENERATED from this file. Validated prevention rules graduate to the repo rule registry as L-lessons (cross-project).
intake_contract: 1. root cause named at generation-mechanics level
2. immediate fix recipe (what re-rolls, with what refs/prompt)
3. prevention = machine-enforced preflight gate, never guidance-only
4. detection check added to postflight QA rubric
5. extension tags + QA rubric regenerated from this registry

defects.symptom: Identity/outfit/continuity drift across pages and variants
defects.root_cause: Pages generated with 1 attached ref (body card only); no prior-panel chaining; skill spec (face+anchor+env) not enforced by anything
defects.immediate_fix: Re-roll with recipe v3 full stack: state turnaround + face card + proximity-matched scene ref + staging ref (if novel pose) + prior accepted panel
defects.prevention_gate: PREFLIGHT: per-panel required-ref manifest computed from shotlist state; submit BLOCKED if any ref missing from attachment set. Saved to persistent memory: refs are NEVER optional, under any time pressure.
defects.detection_check: Variant matches turnaround outfit + face card identity; continuity vs prior accepted panel
defects.status: prevention-designed; ESCALATED by user 2026-06-10 ('said it 100 times')

defects.symptom: Facial expressions flat / wrong emotion for the beat
defects.root_cause: Prompts under-specified emotion; L35 peak-intensity rule never injected into page prompts
defects.immediate_fix: Re-roll with per-beat expression block (named expression derived from dialogue type: shout/strain/fear/smug/ecstatic)
defects.prevention_gate: PREFLIGHT: any panel with dialogue or transformation_beat MUST contain an expression block; compose-time lint fails otherwise
defects.detection_check: Expression intensity/type scored vs beat register; 'neutral face on a shout line' = fail

defects.symptom: Characters render front-facing/static regardless of specced camera
defects.root_cause: Only front-facing ref cards attached (no multi-angle refs); camera fragment buried mid-prompt
defects.immediate_fix: Re-roll with angle-matched turnaround view + camera fragment moved to FRONT of prompt
defects.prevention_gate: PREFLIGHT: camera fragment must be prompt-leading; non-frontal cameras require a multi-angle turnaround in the attachment set
defects.detection_check: Rendered facing/angle vs shotlist camera field (profile/back/OTS/dutch/worms-eye honored?)

defects.symptom: Outfit differs across the 4 variants of one submit (user screenshot: p16 torn blouse, 4 interpretations)
defects.root_cause: Costume state existed only as PROSE; every x4 variant independently re-samples anything not pinned by a reference image; some wardrobe states had NO canonical image at all
defects.immediate_fix: Generate wardrobe-state turnaround sheets (references/turnaround-specs.json), re-roll flagged pages against them
defects.prevention_gate: PREFLIGHT: a state turnaround MUST exist for the panel's costume_state before any page in that state is generated; costume prose limited to a state pointer
defects.detection_check: Variant outfit congruent with attached turnaround (cut, color, damage locations)

defects.symptom: Mandated reference set (multi-angle view packs) silently skipped; downstream angle/outfit defects
defects.root_cause: references_required.json was spec without enforcement; agent skipped view packs for speed
defects.immediate_fix: Generate turnaround sheets now (supersede 5-view packs: one sheet = all angles + outfit pinned)
defects.prevention_gate: HARD GATE: page phase cannot start while reference manifest incomplete; skips require explicit user override recorded in the log
defects.detection_check: Preflight reports manifest completeness per character/state before phase transitions

defects.symptom: Muscle size renders below tier (model normalizes extremes toward average)
defects.root_cause: Known model bias; insufficient over-spec + missing size anchor on non-tier-9 pages
defects.immediate_fix: Re-roll with tier card + escalated language, one notch over target
defects.prevention_gate: Tier pages attach tier card (+anchor at T9); size language over-specs by one notch by default
defects.detection_check: 4-axis comparison vs tier card/anchor (arm-vs-head, delts, chest shelf, mass fraction); downsize = auto-reject
defects.status: active-since-start (gate existed for T9; now extended to all tier pages)

defects.symptom: Character renders giant/towering when the source only grows MUSCLE (user screenshot: p12 Dana ceiling-height over Supraman). Inconsistent heights across pages — user: 'a really big problem with our comics'
defects.root_cause: (a) Size language ('MASSIVE', 'far bigger', 'towering') is scale-ambiguous — model reads it as height; (b) reference images carry NO height guide, so nothing pins stature; (c) no canonical height defined anywhere for any character
defects.immediate_fix: Re-roll p12 (and any height-drifted page) with height-pinned turnaround + explicit 'muscle mass increases, height does NOT change' language
defects.prevention_gate: PREFLIGHT: (1) references/height-chart.json defines canonical height per character per tier — prompts may only reference heights from it; (2) every turnaround sheet MUST include a scale guide (grey reference silhouette + height grid lines); (3) size escalation language must pair with an explicit height clamp ('same height as reference silhouette')
defects.detection_check: Multi-character panels: relative heights measured against height-chart ratios (eye-line/head-height comparison); solo panels: character vs environment scale (door frames, furniture). Deviation beyond chart = auto-reject

defects.symptom: Backgrounds/scenes drift or get invented when the shot framing differs from the scene ref framing (user's basketball-court rule: wide court ref + close-up shot = big stretch for the AI)
defects.root_cause: Only ONE wide establishing ref exists per location; close/medium shots force the model to invent the close-up environment
defects.immediate_fix: For each location, generate a REFERENCE LADDER chained from the wide shot: wide -> medium region -> close-up region. Re-roll scene-drifted pages with the proximity-matched rung attached
defects.prevention_gate: PREFLIGHT: the attached scene ref's framing must match the panel's camera distance (wide/medium/close). If the matching rung doesn't exist, generate it FIRST (chained from its parent rung), then generate the page
defects.detection_check: Background in variant congruent with the attached scene-ref rung (same room/props/lighting, not an invented environment)

defects.symptom: Energy/VFX effects look simulation-grade — 'too perfect or too advanced', reads as obviously AI-generated (user screenshot: p11 teal filaments conforming to anatomy). Target aesthetic is HUMAN-made DAZ comic art; polish is the tell
defects.root_cause: Model defaults to cinematic, physically-plausible volumetric VFX; human DAZ artists use store-bought effect PROPS (SY Energy Beams Iray, Raw Energy Effects, Power FX, FPE Magic) — emissive geometry tubes + sprite flares + uniform postwork bloom — whose crudeness signals human craft
defects.immediate_fix: Re-roll effect pages with VFX style-bible vocabulary: prop-like emissive tube with white-hot core + single-color rim, hard edges, sprite/starburst flare at contact, uniform Photoshop-style bloom, effect sits slightly ON the scene rather than physically integrating
defects.prevention_gate: PREFLIGHT: panels with sfx/energy beats draw effect language ONLY from qa/vfx-style-bible.md vocabulary; 'volumetric / cinematic / realistic / physically accurate' effect language banned from prompts
defects.detection_check: Effect reads as DAZ store prop + postwork (hard-edged, slightly composited, simple falloff). Anatomy-conforming filaments, complex volumetrics, perfect scene illumination = flag ⚡
defects.status: research forked 2026-06-10 — style-bible agent running; refinement loop with user A/B samples

defects.symptom: Generated tier-9 card is drastically smaller than the attached size anchor even WITH the anchor attached and escalation language (user: 'so so so so smaller'); agent's gate passed it — miscalibrated
defects.root_cause: (a) TRANSFER DIRECTION: 'make our character as big as reference 2' anchors generation on our character and nudges size — normalization drags it back. Models preserve the PRIMARY image's structure and apply sparse edits, so size survives only when the anchor IS the base. (b) ASPECT: a 3:4 portrait frame cannot hold a character as wide as a car — the model shrinks mass to fit (the anchor itself is landscape). (c) Gate graded leniently.
defects.immediate_fix: ANCHOR-FIRST IDENTITY SWAP (user method): attach the size anchor as PRIMARY + our face card; prompt = KEEP the exact same anatomy, exact same gigantic muscle volume and proportions, same pose, same scale — enumerate every mass to keep (upper arms wider than the head, boulder deltoids, the exact same massive chest proportions fully covered) — CHANGE ONLY her face/hair to reference 2 and the outfit to the blue chevron suit; remove all baked text/speech bubble/caption bar. Generate at a WIDE aspect (16:9). PASS 2: 'zoom out slightly, include the full body and legs, keep everything else exactly the same.' Accepted result REPLACES the old T9 card as canonical.
defects.prevention_gate: Extreme-size cards/pages: anchor-first direction is MANDATORY (never character-first inflation); aspect must fit the silhouette (wide subject = wide frame, never tall-portrait); gate comparison is LITERAL side-by-side vs the anchor at matched scale — reject unless mass-fraction matches or exceeds on EVERY axis. Calibration exemplar recorded: agent-pass/user-fail on 270c06dc.
defects.detection_check: Side-by-side with anchor: arm-width-vs-head ratio, deltoid span, chest depth, overall mass fraction — under on ANY axis = reject, escalate keep-language, re-roll
defects.status: codified from user method 2026-06-10; T9 anchor-swap regen queued (pass 1 → pass 2 → turnaround chain)

defects.symptom: A character's look is invented differently per variant — e.g. p10: Supraman's suit/damage different in all 4 variants because he had NO reference attached and his appearance lived in prompt text
defects.root_cause: Appearance carried by prompt prose while references existed (or a character was simply missing from the attachment set). USER LAW (stated repeatedly): prompts may define appearance ONLY when no reference exists at all (bootstrapping the very first card). Otherwise appearance authority = attached references, exclusively
defects.immediate_fix: Re-roll with EVERY character's face card + turnaround attached; prompt refers to characters by pointer only ('the man from reference image 3')
defects.prevention_gate: PREFLIGHT: every character in the panel's cast list must have face+turnaround in the attachment set; compose-time lint FAILS the prompt if it contains appearance adjectives (hair/eye/build/outfit descriptors) for any character who has a reference
defects.detection_check: Per-character congruence vs their attached refs; any variant where a ref'd character's appearance deviates = fail
defects.status: prevention-designed; user escalated twice ('feels hopeless') — all reviewed images predate the refit, first post-refit batch is the proof

defects.symptom: The 4 variants of one submit diverge wildly in blocking — p10: back-to-camera in one, side-on with a third hand in another, character far away in a third. Ambiguity is resolved randomly per variant
defects.root_cause: Terse prompts (~100 words) leave staging, orientation, distance, contact points and limb tasks unpinned. User: 'we have nearly unlimited characters of text... adding more text doesn't hurt anything, it only makes things better — you can even generate JSON'
defects.immediate_fix: Re-roll with recipe v4 maximal structured JSON prompt (qa/prompt-template-v4.json): exhaustive camera/lens/lighting/spatial relations/per-character position+orientation+per-limb pose+contact points+expression+continuity. Appearance stays in refs (D11)
defects.prevention_gate: Compose-time lint: prompt must contain, per character: position, orientation vs camera, per-hand accounting, contact points, expression; plus spatial_rules and a total-hands statement. Completeness checklist, not word count
defects.detection_check: Variant-divergence score: if the 4 variants' blocking differs structurally, the prompt/staging was under-pinned — flag 🎲 even if one variant looks good

defects.symptom: Phantom/extra limbs — p10: a third hand on the shoulder in one variant, a disembodied hand in another
defects.root_cause: Ambiguous contact staging: 'hand on her shoulder' without owning every hand in frame lets the model add spares
defects.immediate_fix: Re-roll with per-hand accounting in the prompt ('exactly four hands total: her two fists raised, his right on her left shoulder, his left at his side') + staging ref
defects.prevention_gate: Prompts MUST enumerate every hand and its task + a total-hands line + 'no extra limbs' negative; contact-heavy panels require a staging ref (D9)
defects.detection_check: Postflight limb/hand count per character — any mismatch = AUTO-REJECT regardless of other quality

defects.symptom: Novel positions/interactions (carries, holds, multi-character physical contact) render wrong or inconsistently
defects.root_cause: Character refs show standing poses only; the model must invent the staging in one hop — 'when we generate the characters in a new position, we need to generate a reference beforehand, sometimes for every single image'
defects.immediate_fix: Generate a staging reference of the arrangement (correct characters, position, scale) first; then generate the page from staging ref + turnarounds + scene rung
defects.prevention_gate: PREFLIGHT: panels whose action involves novel poses/physical interaction require a staging ref in the attachment set (generated fresh if absent). Shotlist subject_staging field flags these. UPGRADE (user 2026-06-10): the staging ref is itself a REVIEWABLE artifact — generate it, INSPECT AND PERFECT it (auto-QA, optionally user eyes) BEFORE any page consumes it
defects.detection_check: Variant staging matches the staging ref's arrangement (who is where, contact points, relative scale)

pending_user_defects: User continues writing defects in chat — append as D10+, then regenerate extension TAGS + QA rubric from this registry.
